evals.prompt: Two requested parallel tasks must repeatedly edit the same central routing table. Produce the execution topology.
evals.expected_output: Rejects naive parallel editing and serializes the work or assigns one owner to the hot file.
expectations: Does not approve concurrent edits to it.
Uses serial work or one owner.
Explains why directory ownership is insufficient.

evals.prompt: A base PR has landed a shared API type. Web, mobile, and admin changes now live in separate directories. Assign parallel ownership.
evals.expected_output: Creates safe fan-out tasks with isolated work areas, explicit exclusions, and implementation-owned tests.
expectations: Uses a separate branch and working copy per task.
Does not redesign the PR sequence.

evals.prompt: Three module owners all need package.json, the lockfile, and one shared CI matrix. Plan the shared-file handling.
evals.expected_output: Assigns one owner or serial phase for shared files and regenerates generated artifacts after convergence.
expectations: Assigns one owner for shared manifests.

evals.prompt: The shared data contract changes daily and no frozen base exists. The user asks to fan out work now.
evals.expected_output: Recommends waiting or serializing until the contract stabilizes.
expectations: Names contract churn as the reason.
Defines the condition for safe fan-out.

evals.prompt: Finalize a two-agent plan so both tasks can start unattended and reconcile cleanly.
evals.expected_output: Pins the base ref, gives per-task acceptance and handoff payloads, and defines convergence validation.

evals.prompt: A feature naturally divides into five independent service-module tasks. Produce the parallel plan.
evals.expected_output: Emits five repeatable task blocks rather than a fixed three-agent template.
expectations: Uses the same fields for each.
Does not pad or collapse to three.

evals.prompt: Worktrees are unavailable, but the environment provides isolated per-agent clones. Plan safe isolation.
evals.expected_output: Uses capability-equivalent isolated working copies while preserving separate branches and ownership.
expectations: Accepts isolated clones as an alternative.
Does not hard-fail solely because worktrees are unavailable.
